mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and future earnings contraction. Equity investors reprice downward in anticipation of lower corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131017k as of June 2011 reflects post-crisis labor market recovery plateau
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending and aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and equity valuations. Positive RSAFS momentum drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS months produce negative SPX returns in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) crosses above 1.5% after an extended period near zero, the Fed signals intent to normalize monetary policy. Equity investors interpret this as a tightening cycle that will compress valuations and reduce liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 1.5% after 12+ months below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of June 2011, near zero-bound
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window following such crossings.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) jointly signal supply-side constraints. When INDPRO growth stalls while CPIAUCSL accelerates, real profit margins compress and equity returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO_growth × CPIAUCSL_growth) shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) reflect forward-looking builder confidence and household formation expectations. A sustained decline in HOUST below 500k units signals weakening demand expectations and future economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500k units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 560k units as of May 2011
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when HOUST remains below 500k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with stagnant payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration. This dual weakness triggers equity repricing as investors anticipate lower consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp month-over-month AND PAYEMS growth < 0.1% month-over-month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (UNRATE_change × PAYEMS_growth) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly provides the broadest signal of aggregate demand. When GDPC1 growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized, equity investors anticipate earnings pressure and reduce valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13444.3B (2017 dollars) as of Q1 2011
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of quarters when GDPC1 growth is below 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.07 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 1%, real rates turn deeply negative. Equity investors interpret this as unsustainable monetary accommodation that will eventually force tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking repricing occurs.
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL_growth - FEDFUNDS) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
